steps.title: Spot the contrast structure
steps.content: The strongest signal is the poem's contrasting structure: while outside conditions are chaotic (“tempest is raving,” “ship is madly driving”), Puerto Cabello's waters are serene (“warm,” “easy motion,” “gently glides”). This opposition is the core of the passage.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly identifies this opposition: Puerto Cabello's tranquil waters against the rough waters outside.

The other three miss the water contrast entirely, each pursuing a different misreading: an incoming storm (B), boat types (C), or people never mentioned (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
